# Baloo — pre-tune diff (maiden prep), 2026-07-11
# Generated by tools/pretune.py, then curated. Diffed against
# INAV_9.0.1_cli_20260421_210721.txt (bare INAV 9.0 defaults).
#
# Strategy for the first-ever flight of this airframe:
#   - Apply only the physics-exact nav/cruise/launch numbers autotune never
#     sets (these are what stall a maiden RTH). Currently all unset.
#   - Leave the rate PIDs at INAV defaults; fly gently in ANGLE, then engage
#     AUTO TUNE (SF) once trimmed. Do NOT paste guessed PIDs on a maiden.
#   - Bank capped to 35° (INAV default) for a wide, gentle loiter.
#
# Command:  tools/pretune.py baloo --diff --pid none --bank 35
#
# CAVEATS before flying:
#   - Set CG to ~28% MAC = 64 mm behind the wing LE (FT Sea Duck spec), then
#     confirm with a glide test. Nothing here can fix a tail-heavy plane.
#   - Baloo is an amphibious twin — it takes off on water/ground roll, not a
#     hand chuck. NAV LAUNCH's steep-climb detection is aimed at hand/bungee
#     launches, so the launch_* lines below may be moot; skip them if you're
#     not using NAV LAUNCH (SD).
#   - cruise_throttle 1393 (~39%) assumes T/W≈1.1 and a 0.6 forward-thrust
#     derate; verify against a level-flight throttle once flown.

set nav_fw_cruise_speed = 1052
set fw_reference_airspeed = 1052
set nav_fw_bank_angle = 35
set nav_fw_loiter_radius = 1610
set nav_fw_cruise_throttle = 1393
set nav_fw_min_throttle = 1150
set nav_fw_max_throttle = 1850
set nav_fw_launch_climb_angle = 34
set nav_fw_launch_max_angle = 45
set nav_fw_launch_thr = 1700
set battery_capacity = 2200

# CG target: 28% MAC (64 mm from LE) — set physically, not in software.
save
